steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side
steps.content:
Left of the blank: a complete main clause ending with the quoted Nobel criterion — “produced the most outstanding work in an idealistic direction”.

Right of the blank: “judges recognized Lagerlöf [for] the lofty idealism, vivid imagination and spiritual perception that characterize her writings” — another independent clause, even with its introductory time phrase “in 1909, for instance.”

The blank requires punctuation after the closing quotation mark that properly connects these two complete thoughts.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses when they are closely related.

These two clauses are: the first states the Nobel criterion (authors must produce idealistic work); the second provides a concrete historical example of judges recognizing that criterion.

Grammatically incorrect punctuation after the closing quotation mark fails, and leaving no punctuation between the two related independent clauses leaves them improperly separated.

The semicolon properly shows that the example supports and illustrates the criterion.

That is the correct answer.
